title: Coordinate Geometry: Distance Formula
learningOutcomes: Master the core principles of Coordinate Geometry: Distance Formula.

objectives.text: Adjust the distance between points A and B to exactly 5 units.

theory: The distance formula is derived from Pythagoras theorem: d = sqrt((x2 - x1)^2 + (y2 - y1)^2). it computes the straight line distance between any two coordinates.

tasks.instruction: Adjust the distance between points A and B to exactly 5 units.

observationPrompts: How did coordinates react during drag movements?
